title: Local Input Security, Resource Limits, Privacy, and Diagnostics · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for local input security, resource limits, privacy, and diagnostics and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, Tcl, Tk, display, input, assistive-technology, file, package, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded local-data threat model and controls. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Identify untrusted files, dimensions, strings, paths, package resources, environment state, logs, and denial-of-service consequences.
Use bounded data-only formats and reject pickle or executable object reconstruction for untrusted saves.
Validate dimensions, cell counts, edge counts, nesting, string length, numeric ranges, and total work before allocation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Privacy Diagnostics

Build deterministic Python evidence to bound local inputs, reject executable serialization, constrain resources, and minimize diagnostics; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Local Threat Model, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Local Threat Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Local Threat Model, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A quiz puzzle team handles case 3226 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound local inputs, reject executable serialization, constrain resources, and minimize diagnostics; the prior good state must survive failure; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Identify untrusted files, dimensions, strings, paths, package resources, environment state, logs, and denial-of-service consequences.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Local Threat Model, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Offline desktop software has no meaningful attack surface.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Privacy Diagnostics
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Privacy Diagnostics: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz puzzle team handles case 4845 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound local inputs, reject executable serialization, constrain resources, and minimize diagnostics; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Collect minimal redacted diagnostics with consent, bounded retention, export control, and deletion while preserving causal evidence.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Privacy Diagnostics.
steps.why: Retrieving Privacy Diagnostics through the concrete bounded local-data threat model and controls case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Privacy Diagnostics, keep Privacy Diagnostics connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Collect minimal redacted diagnostics with consent, bounded retention, export control, and deletion while preserving causal evidence.
